content.full_text:
Fern PolrrrcAl Pnecrrcss C oMMrssroN
428 J Street . Suire 620 r Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 32?-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 26,2008
Joanne Chapman, RN
Regional Trauma Clinical Coordinator
Coastal Valleys EMS Agency
475 Aviation Blvd. Suite 200
Santa Rosa, CA 95403
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-08-154
Dear Ms. Chapman:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 m aaAitlon, we base this letter on the
facts presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act
as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesblt (1g75) 1 FppC Ops. 71.)
Please note that we base our advice solely on the provisions of the Act and offer no
advice on how other state laws or local laws might affect you.
QUESTION
Does your previous position with St. Joseph,s Health System as a per diem
registered nurse present a disqualifying conflict of interest for iecision, yo,, ,rroy make
for your current employer, the Coastal Valleys Emergency Medical Services Agency?
CONCLUSION
lnterest
interest
You do not currently have a decision before you that would present a conflict of
. For this reason, as discussed below, there is no cllrrent disqualifying conflict of
FACTS
You are currently employed with the County of Sonoma as the Regional
Trauma/Clinical Coordinator for the Coastal Valleys Emergency Medical Services
Agency, a regional regulatory agency that oversees pre-hospital medical services for
Sonoma, Napa' and Mendocino Counties. Your duties include oversight of the counties.
I The Political Retirrm Act is contained in Government code Secrions li 1000 through 9l()14. All
stattlk)ry ret'erences are kr the Government Code. unless otherrvise indieated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Conlmission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through ltt997 of Title 2 of the califirrnia
Cttde of Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Caliturnia Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicatecl.


File No. I-08-151
Page No. 2
trallma centers and plans, quality assllrance, and disbursement of state trauma care funds
when available, among other duties.
The County has begun discussing whether to re-designate Santa Rosa Memorial
Hospital as a Trauma Center. Your county counsel determined that you have a
disqualifying conflict of interest regarding that decision, and you are not participating in
any aspect of that determination. Your qLlestion involves your cllrrent job duties.
AI\ALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials "perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused bytheir own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).)
Specifically, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, parricipating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influenc" o gor.rnrn"ntol
decision in which the official has a financial interest.
The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis to decide whether an
official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regulation tgTObGXl)-(g).) The
general rule, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes
a governmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one
or more of his or her financial interests. (Section 87103.)
Step One: Are you a public official?
As an employee of Sonoma County, you are a public official under the Act.
(Section 82048(a).)
step Two: will you be making, participating in making, or using or
attempting to use your official position to influence a governmental deciiion?
You have described your job duties as a Regional Trauma/Clinical Coordinator.
Because you have not stated a decision that will be before you in this capacity, we cannot
say whether you will be making, participating in making, or using or attempting to use
your official position to influence a decision.
As we discussed during out phone call, you are welcome to write in again should
you have a decision before you that could potentially create a disqualifying c[nflict of
interest.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
&A"lrt'W'r^'*
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
